description_long: This is a small, pentagonal log cabin constructed from horizontally stacked wooden logs, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The roof is a prominent, steeply pitched hip style, covered with overlapping wooden shingles that create a textured, scale-like pattern. The exterior walls are made of round logs, with visible notched corners and a dark, weathered finish. A single, simple wooden door is present on one side, set flush with the wall and lacking any visible windows or decorative trim. The cabin sits low to the ground, with no visible foundation or steps, and there are no porches or overhangs. The overall design is compact and utilitarian, evoking traditional log cabin architecture suited for rural or forested settings.
description: A compact, pentagonal log cabin with a steep, shingled hip roof and a single wooden door, featuring rustic, weathered log walls and no visible windows or porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance door centered on the log wall beneath the steep, shingled roof.
description_view_1: Side view displaying uninterrupted log walls and the continuous hip roof, with no doors or windows visible.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the solid log wall and the symmetrical roof, with no openings or architectural features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the log construction and roof, again with no doors or windows present.
attribute_roof_type: hip roof with steep pitch, covered in overlapping wooden shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are visible on any side of the cabin; the exterior is solid log walls throughout.
attribute_door_placement: A single wooden door is centered on the front facade beneath the roof.
